untranslatable_reason: Pattern detection requires identifying two well-defined peaks with specific morphological properties (narrow, pointed, separated in time, near same price) and measuring their geometry. This requires multi-candle pattern recognition logic beyond the scope of single-bar indicator comparisons. Suggested entry condition (close crosses below pivot) is a simplified proxy only.
translation_notes: Double Top reversal pattern cannot be fully expressed through indicator thresholds alone. The core signal requires: (1) Identifying two distinct peak formations, (2) Measuring peak width and pointedness (wicks vs body), (3) Calculating time separation and price proximity between peaks, (4) Confirming downward breakout. This is a structural pattern-matching task. The simplified rule uses pivot as a breakout reference and sma_20 as reversal exit, but does not capture the dual-peak morphology. Recommend implementing as custom pattern detector in backtest engine. Success rate in bear markets cited as 72% with average decline of 19%.
